questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
In finding out refractive index of glass slab the following observations were made through travelling microscope 50 vernier scale division $=49 \mathrm{MSD} ; 20$ divisions on main scale in each $\mathrm{cm}$
For mark on paper
$\mathrm{MSR}=8.45 \mathrm{~cm}, \mathrm{VC}=26$
For mark on paper seen through slab
MSR $=7.12 \mathrm{~cm}, V C=41$
For powder particle on the top surface of the glass slab
$\mathrm{MSR}=4.05 \mathrm{~cm}, \mathrm{VC}=1$
(MSR $=$ Main Scale Reading, VC $=$ Vernier Coincidence)
Refractive index of the glass slab is :
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & 1 \mathrm{MSD}=\frac{1 \mathrm{~cm}}{20}=0.05 \mathrm{~cm} \\ & 1 \mathrm{VSD}=\frac{49}{50} \mathrm{MSD}=\frac{49}{50} \times 0.05 \mathrm{~cm}=0.049 \mathrm{~cm} \\ & \mathrm{LC}=1 \mathrm{MSD}-1 \mathrm{VSD}=0.001 \mathrm{~cm} \end{aligned}$
For mark on paper, $\mathrm{L}_1=8.45 \mathrm{~cm}+26 \times 0.001 \mathrm{~cm}$ $=84.76 \mathrm{~mm}$ For mark on paper through slab, $\mathrm{L} 2=7.12 \mathrm{~cm}+$ $41 \times 0.001 \mathrm{~cm}=71.61 \mathrm{~mm}$ For powder particle on top surface, $\mathrm{ZE}=4.05 \mathrm{~cm}$ $+1 \times 0.001 \mathrm{~cm}=40.51 \mathrm{~mm}$
$\begin{aligned} \therefore & \text { actual } \mathrm{L}_1=84.76-40.51=44.25 \mathrm{~mm} \\ & \text { actual } \mathrm{L}_2=71.61-40.51=31.10 \mathrm{~mm} \\ & \mathrm{~L}_2=\frac{\mathrm{L}_1}{\mu} \\ \Rightarrow & \mu=\frac{\mathrm{L}_1}{\mathrm{~L}_2}=\frac{44.25}{31.10}=1.42\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: The refractive index of prism is $\mu=\sqrt{3}$ and the ratio of the angle of minimum deviation to the angle of prism is one. The value of angle of prism is _______.
questions.solution:
For $\delta_{\min }$ $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{i}=\mathrm{e} \\ & \mathrm{r}_1=\mathrm{r}_2=\frac{\mathrm{A}}{2} \\ & \frac{\delta_{\min }}{\mathrm{A}}=1 \\ & \frac{2 \mathrm{i}-\mathrm{A}}{\mathrm{A}}=1 \\ & \\ & 2 \mathrm{i}=2 \mathrm{~A} \\ & \mathrm{i}=\mathrm{A} \end{aligned}$
Snell's law $\begin{aligned} & 1 \times \sin i=\mu \sin r \\ & \sin i=\mu \sin \left(\frac{A}{2}\right) \\ & \sin A=\mu \sin \left(\frac{A}{2}\right) \\ & 2 \sin \frac{A}{2} \cos \frac{A}{2}=\sqrt{3} \sin \left(\frac{A}{2}\right) \\ & \cos \left(\frac{A}{2}\right)=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \\ & \therefore \frac{A}{2}=30^{\circ} \\ & \therefore A=60^{\circ} \end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A light ray is incident on a glass slab of thickness $4 \sqrt{3} \mathrm{~cm}$ and refractive index $\sqrt{2}$ . The angle of incidence is equal to the critical angle for the glass slab with air. The lateral displacement of ray after passing through glass slab is _____ $\mathrm{cm}$. $\left(\right.$ Given $\left.\sin 15^{\circ}=0.25\right)$
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & i=\theta_c \\ & \Rightarrow i=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{\mu}\right) \\ & \Rightarrow i=45^{\circ} \end{aligned}$ and according to snell's law $\begin{aligned} & 1 \sin 45^{\circ}=\sqrt{2} \sin \mathrm{r} \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{r}=30^{\circ} \end{aligned}$
Lateral displacement $\Delta=\frac{\mathrm{t} \sin (\mathrm{i}-\mathrm{r})}{\cos \mathrm{r}}$ $\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \Delta=\frac{4 \sqrt{3} \times \sin 15^{\circ}}{\cos 30^{\circ}} \\ & \Rightarrow \Delta=2 \mathrm{~cm} \end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: The position of the image formed by the combination of lenses is :
questions.solution:
For lens $1: \mathrm{f}_1=10, \mathrm{u}=-30, \mathrm{v}=$ ? $v=\frac{u f}{u+f}=\frac{-30 \times 10}{-30+10}=15$ For lens 2: $\mathrm{f}_1=-10, \mathrm{u}=10, \mathrm{v}=$ ? $v=\frac{u f}{u+f}=\frac{10 \times-10}{10-10}=\infty$
For lens $3: \mathrm{f}=30, \mathrm{u}=-\infty, \mathrm{v}=$ ? So v will be 30 .

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A convex mirror of radius of curvature <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>30</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> forms an image that is half the size of the object. The object distance is :
questions.solution:
Given R=30 cm

So, f=R2=+15 cm

For convex mirror, virtual image is formed for real object. Thus, magnification m=12

The magnification can be written as

m=-vu   ...1

Equation (1) implies that

12=-vu⇒v=-u2

The relation between the object distance, image distance and the focal length of the mirror is given by

1f=1v+1u   ...2

Equation (2) implies that

115=1-u2+1u=-1u⇒u=-15 cm

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: If the refractive index of the material of a prism is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>cot</mi><mfenced><mfrac><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac></mfenced></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> is the angle of prism then the angle of minimum deviation will be
questions.solution:
The formula to calculate the refractive index μ of the material of the prism is given by

μ=sinA+δm2sinA2   ...1

From equation (1), it follows that

cotA2=sinA+δm2sinA2

⇒cosA2=sinA+δm2⇒sinπ2-A2=sinA+δm2

⇒A+δm2=π2-A2

⇒δm=π-2 A

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A biconvex lens of refractive index <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn></math> has a focal length of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>20</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> in air. Its focal length when immersed in a liquid of refractive index <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>6</mn></math> will be:
questions.solution:
Given, the refractive index of the material of the lens is μ1=1.5, the refractive index of the medium is μm=1.6 and the focal length of the lens in air is fa=20 cm.

In air:

1fa=μ1-12R

In liquid:

1fm=μ1μm-12R

The focal length as measured in air and that as measured inside the liquid medium are related by, 

fmfa=μ1-1μmμ1-μm   ...1

Equation (1) implies that

fm20=(1.5-1)1.6(1.5-1.6)⇒fm=-160 cm

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: In an experiment to measure the focal length (f) of a convex lens, the magnitude of object distance(x) and the image distance(y) are measured with reference to the focal point of the lens. The y-x plot is shown in figure. The focal length of the lens is _____cm.
questions.solution:
From Newton's formula for lens, we can write

x1x2=f2⇒20×20=f2

Therefore, f=20 cm
